Description: 
Join us for an insightful conversation between India's Foreign Minister, Dr. S. Jaishankar, and Cummins CEO, Jennifer Rumsey, as they discuss Cummins' remarkable contributions to India's growth and development. Dr. Jaishankar acknowledges and appreciates Cummins' role in powering India's progress and emphasizes the importance of fostering future investments and cooperative endeavors. Explore how collaboration between industry and diplomacy paves the way for a stronger, more sustainable India. Don't miss this engaging exchange of ideas and vision for a brighter future!

snippet.title: President Biden Tours Cummins' Green Hydrogen Production Site and Supports Clean Energy Investments
snippet.description:
President Biden recently made a visit to Cummins' plant in Fridley, Minnesota, to commemorate the passage of the Inflation Reduction Act and its impact on the clean energy economy. During his tour of the site, the President observed the progress of Accelera by Cummins' first U.S.-based production facility, which will soon be manufacturing electrolyzers, a vital component in the production of green hydrogen. Cummins' announcement of over $1 billion in investments for enhancements to its U.S. engine manufacturing network, with a focus on evolving low-carbon fuel capabilities such as natural gas and diesel, as well as hydrogen, was welcomed by Jennifer Rumsey, Cummins' President and CEO, who showed her support in forums along with the President, Secretary of Treasury Janet Yellen, and General Motors CEO Mary Barra. Hydrogen produced with renewable energy sources is referred to as "green" hydrogen, and can be used as a clean power source to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors, with the electrolyzer being an essential technology in its production.

snippet.title: Accelera by Cummins to look zero-emissions solutions
snippet.description: In an exclusive studio interview from the Canadian Hydrogen Convention 2023, Julian Walker sat down with Alex Savelli, Managing Director of Americas for Hydrogen Technologies, Accelera by Cummins, to discuss the new brand for its new power business unit. We hear how Accelera will focus on a range of zero-emissions solutions to allow industries accelerate their transition to a sustainable future. Alex also touched on expanding the company’s electrolyzer manufacturing footprint to the United States and Canada.

snippet.title: The autonomous future is nearly here - with Wejo | Sponsored
snippet.description:
Widespread use of autonomous cars is on the horizon. Self-driving vehicles are already out on our roads. And autonomy will change our relationship with our vehicles. But what will the new immersive world inside a vehicle be like?

Read more here:

In the third episode of a three-part special series partnered with Wejo, the smart mobility tech company, a panel of expert guests discuss how legislation and policy are enabling self-driving vehicles – and how AVs will change our lives. Will our children do their homework in the car on their way to school? Will we watch films on long journeys on the motorway?

Alona Ferber, editor of the New Statesman’s Spotlight policy channel, is joined by Richard Barlow, founder and chief executive of Wejo, Larry Burns, the former corporate vice president of Research and Development for General Motors who championed self-driving and electric vehicles – and now sits on Wejo’s board – Alex Kendall, CEO of AV2.0 start-up Wayve, and Amanda Stretton, the former racing driver, broadcaster, and automotive expert.

“It's pretty much like sitting in a regular vehicle, whether your spouse or a taxi cab driver is driving it,” Burns says on the episode, when asked what it’s like to travel in a self-driving car. “I think that's what's so remarkable. Once you get by the novelty of the fact that there's no one sitting in the driver's seat, you realise that the car is doing pretty much everything that a human has to do to drive.”

Burns goes on to describe how autonomous vehicles will change people’s lives: “The really exciting thing is it's not just about not having to drive or spend your time driving anymore. I think it's the transformation and how we're going to be living our lives both socially and economically, having a vehicle that you can dispatch yourself and it serves as your personal valet to run errands for you, I think is going to be a game changer for many of us.

Not having to spend your time driving and being able to reconfigure the interior of a vehicle would allow the vehicle to become a play area. It could become a place to sleep, it could become a sanctuary…I think the sky's the limit quite honestly.”

The first episode in this series looked at connected vehicle data and how far we have come on electric vehicles.
--

snippet.title: Is China's Economy A Time Bomb Ready To Explode?
snippet.description:
Chinese Economy Challenges: China's economy is facing a series of challenges, including slow growth, record youth unemployment, dwindling foreign investment, weak exports, and a property sector crisis.

Political Perspectives: U.S. President Joe Biden has referred to China's economy as a "ticking time bomb," anticipating internal discontent. However, China's leader, Xi Jinping, defends the nation's economy, emphasizing its resilience and potential.

Property Market Crisis: The property market, a significant contributor to China's wealth for two decades, is in crisis due to the global pandemic and a shrinking population. Government restrictions on developer borrowing have led to a debt spiral, plummeting property prices, and a stalled economic recovery.

Construction Addiction: China exhibits an addiction to construction, exemplified by the decision to build a multi-million dollar COVID-19 quarantine facility despite financial strain. Local governments in debt are resorting to selling land to fund relentless building programs, indicating a need for a shift in economic strategy.

Future Economic Path: China's economy is at a crossroads, requiring structural and institutional reforms. Experts suggest a shift from the current construction-heavy model to developing a robust financial sector. However, this transition is hindered by government regulations and control over the finance sector, posing a challenge for China's economic evolution.

snippet.title: Accelera By Cummins, Freightliner's eM2, International's EV ecosystem and Peterbilt's hydrogen  plan
snippet.description:
Decarbonization in trucking is happening, but there's no magic bullet to lowering emissions. That's what makes ACT Expo so exciting--the truck equipment and technology of tomorrow is on display. Cleaner diesel, natural gas, hydrogen ICE, battery electric, hydrogen fuel cells--it's all here. We decided to bring it all to you in our FE From the Floor video series that talks to the biggest names in the industry and kicks the sustainability tires of trucking's latest environmentally-minded solutions that aim to bring real ROI to fleet operations. Watch the first episode of FE From the Floor: ACT Expo 2023 above and stay tuned tomorrow and Friday for more episodes.
